Title: MBPP culls over 4,000 pest birds in May control operations
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Island City Council (MBPP) culled 4,424 pest birds and destroyed 125 crow nests last month as part of efforts to safeguard public health and urban cleanliness.
MBPP said it remained committed to maintaining a clean, safe and healthy urban environment through continuous pest bird control operations across its jurisdiction.
"During the month, a total of 1,817 pigeons were culled through sustained control operations. The council also culled 2,607 crows and destroyed 125 crow nests to prevent further population growth," it said in a social media post.
MBPP said it had also stepped up enforcement against those feeding wild birds, a key factor contributing to rising pest bird populations.
[[nid:1456231]]
"The council issued 17 compounds under Section 47(1)(g) of the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974 to individuals found feeding birds," it said.
It added that it would continue to intensify control and enforcement operations from time to time to ensure a clean, safe and conducive environment for residents.
The council also reminded the public not to feed wild birds, warning that such actions could worsen pest bird infestations and affect public health and cleanliness.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops plead not guilty to extorting RM10,000 from salesman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Four police corporals pleaded not guilty in the magistrate's court today to a charge of jointly extorting RM10,000 from a salesman by allegedly threatening him with a heavier court fine.
Mohd Al Hafis Sairudin, 43, Mohd Azmi Mohd Ghozali, 40, Muhamad Azlan Zakaria, 39, and Muhammad Hanif Abdul Talib, 30, entered their pleas before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan.
The four allegedly intimidated the 35-year-old salesman by making him believe he faced a minimum court fine of RM50,000, inducing him to hand over RM10,000.
The offence was allegedly committed at noon at an apartment off Jalan Kepong in Sentul on Dec 23, 2023.
They were charged under Section 384 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which carries a maximum jail term of 10 years, a fine, or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail as the offence is non-bailable.
She submitted that if bail were to be granted, it should be set at a high amount with strict conditions.
Defence lawyer Ahmad Ikram Ab Halim urged the court to grant minimal bail, saying his clients had young children and elderly parents who depended on them.
He told the court that the accused understood the bail conditions and would ensure their attendance at future proceedings while cooperating with investigators.
Ahmad Ikram said this was the first time the four men had been charged in court and argued there was no risk of them absconding as they remained in active service.
The magistrate granted each accused bail of RM8,000 with two sureties.
She also ordered them to report to a police station once a month and prohibited them from contacting or harassing witnesses, particularly the victim, failing which their bail would be revoked.
The court fixed July 8 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India beats growth forecasts but Iran war clouds outlook
Article:
MUMBAI: India's economy grew faster than expected in the first three months of 2026, supported by strong government spending and a resilient services sector, although the Middle East conflict has clouded the outlook for the rest of the year.
Gross domestic product expanded 7.8 per cent in the January-March quarter from a year earlier, according to official data released on Friday, exceeding market expectations of 7.3 per cent growth.
Growth for the 2025-26 fiscal year came in at 7.7 per cent, up from 7.1 per cent the previous year, reaffirming India's position as the world's fastest-growing major economy.
However, the Iran war has raised concerns over higher energy costs and inflation, with India heavily reliant on imported oil and gas.
The country's central bank on Friday cut its 2026-27 growth forecast to 6.6 per cent from 6.9 per cent, while policymakers unveiled measures to attract foreign investment and support the rupee after overseas investors sold more than US$20 billion worth of Indian shares this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No nation on its own is able to quickly replace lost Middle East oil supply – Rosneft CEO
Article:
ST PETERSBURG: No country, including the US, could quickly replace oil supplies lost as a result of the Middle East crisis, Rosneft chief executive Igor Sechin said on Friday.
He warned that the market would struggle to offset any significant disruption to crude flows from the region.
Asked whether oil prices could rise to US$150 per barrel if the Strait of Hormuz were closed, Sechin replied: "Ask comrade Trump."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt targets 650,000 TVET students by year-end, says Zahid
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government is targeting 650,000 technical and vocational education and training (TVET) students across 1,395 institutions nationwide by Dec 31, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said about 587,000 students enrolled in TVET institutions last year, compared with about 121,000 when the council was first established.
"This shows that awareness among parents and students about TVET has been increasing, and this five-fold growth is a very encouraging achievement.
"We are aiming for TVET to become the first choice for students, while recognising that academic pathways continue through public and private higher education institutions, with TVET focusing strongly on meeting the demands of the national labour market," he told reporters after a walkabout at National TVET Day 2026 at Dataran Putrajaya.
On National TVET Day, Zahid said the initiative aimed to showcase TVET institutions nationwide and promote high-quality programmes to students and prospective applicants.
He said it was necessary to meet labour market demand, particularly in producing skilled workers for industries requiring local talent.
"This will directly help improve the starting salaries of TVET graduates," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil edges lower after Oman says Mina al Fahal operations proceeding normally
Article:
LONDON: Oil prices edged lower after Oman said operations at Mina al Fahal port were proceeding normally, following a Reuters report that oil loadings had been suspended after an explosion.
Brent crude futures fell by 50 cents, or 0.53 per cent, to US$94.53 a barrel by 0915 GMT after settling down 2.84 per cent in the previous session.
US West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$92.61 a barrel, down 43 cents, or 0.46 per cent, following a 3.1 per cent loss on Thursday.
Both contracts still looked set to post their first weekly gains in three weeks, with Brent up 2.7 per cent and WTI around 6 per cent.
The contracts rose after fighting flared in the Middle East as US-Iran war peace talks dragged on while traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, where a fifth of the world's oil passes, remained limited.
Petroleum Development Oman said on Friday that operations at Mina Al Fahal port were proceeding normally, after three sources told Reuters earlier that oil loading had been suspended following an explosion near its mooring berths.
Oman exports 800,000 to 900,000 barrels per day of crude from the terminal.
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected on Thursday a US-brokered agreement between Israel and the Lebanese government to halt the fighting. Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington.
US President Donald Trump said on Thursday he believed progress was being made between Israel and Lebanon and that Lebanon deserved to have peace.
"Any optimism remains heavily clouded by a tangled web of headlines and counter-headlines," IG market analyst Tony Sycamore said in a note.
OPEC is sticking to its oil demand growth forecast of 1.2 million barrels per day for this year, Secretary General Haitham Al Ghais said on Thursday, despite the Middle East conflict and closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Iranian oil exports have fallen to their lowest level in six years mainly due to the US naval blockade, according to shipping data, although weak demand in China has depressed prices for the oil.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transparency International Malaysia warns against overuse of corruption settlements, 'pay-to-settle' culture
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) has raised concerns over the growing use of compounds, settlements and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases.
Its president, Dr Raymon Ram, said the increasing reliance on such mechanisms without clear public justification could erode trust in anti-corruption institutions and raise questions over accountability.
Raymon said TI-M recognised that Malaysian law provides for compounds and other discretionary mechanisms, and acknowledged the importance of recovering public funds and proceeds of unlawful activities.
"However, we are concerned about the broader implications of resolving corruption-related matters without a full judicial determination of accountability," he said.
Raymon said TI-M had consistently cautioned that such outcomes could undermine public confidence in accountability and the equal application of the law.
He cited several high-profile cases resolved through settlements, asset recovery arrangements or prosecutorial withdrawals, including the discontinuance of a 1MDB-linked money laundering prosecution in 2020 and the discharge of a senior political figure after a RM1.1 million compound in 2021.
"While each case carries its own legal and evidential considerations, the cumulative effect has contributed to a growing public perception that certain corruption and corruption-linked matters are increasingly being resolved through negotiated financial outcomes rather than being subjected to judicial scrutiny," he said.
Raymon noted that the attorney-general, acting as public prosecutor, has broad discretion under Article 145(3) of the Federal Constitution, while certain offences may be compounded under existing laws, including the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA).
However, he said the existence of such legal powers does not diminish the need for transparency, consistency and public confidence in how they are exercised, particularly in corruption-related cases.
"Corruption is not merely a financial loss to be recovered. It is an abuse of entrusted power that undermines public institutions, weakens governance and erodes trust in the rule of law.
"Recovering funds answers the question of what was returned, but it does not necessarily establish accountability, responsibility or deterrence," he said.
Raymon also warned against normalising what many Malaysians increasingly perceive as a "pay-to-settle" culture.
He called for greater transparency from the Attorney-General's Chambers and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission whenever corruption-related charges are discontinued following compounds, settlements or asset recovery arrangements.
He also proposed clearer public guidelines governing the use of such mechanisms, stronger oversight and reporting of discretionary settlement arrangements, and the acceleration of institutional reforms, including separating the roles of the attorney-general and public prosecutor, to strengthen public confidence in prosecutorial decision-making.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Middle East conflict pushing millions into hunger, WFP says
Article:
GENEVA: The Middle East conflict is pushing millions of people closer to hunger, as rising fuel and transport costs drive up food prices while funding shortfalls force aid agencies to scale back assistance, the UN World Food Programme said on Friday.
Joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran in February triggered a regional conflict stretching across the Gulf and into Lebanon, disrupting key shipping routes, including the Strait of Hormuz, forcing vessels to reroute and sharply constraining global energy flows and supply chains.
In March, the WFP forecast as many as 45 million people could fall into acute food insecurity if oil prices remained around US$100 per barrel through June. That scenario is now unfolding, the agency said, with benchmark crude prices staying above that level since early March.
Households in Afghanistan, Somalia and Sri Lanka are among the most seriously affected and face mounting pressure due to higher fuel costs, food price spikes, income losses and disrupted trade.
In Somalia, 6.5 million people, roughly a third of the population, are expected to face severe hunger in 2026, while Afghanistan could see 17.4 million people affected, the WFP said. The situation is projected to worsen, with an additional 2.5 million Somalis and 2.3 million Afghans at risk of falling into food insecurity if disruptions persist. Both countries are reliant on imported energy and food.
### FOOD SHORTAGES TO HIT VERY VULNERABLE CHILDREN
The Middle East crisis comes amid a deep funding shortfall for aid agencies. The WFP said it expected to serve 1.5 million fewer people globally in 2026, and 9 million fewer if the situation persists for six months.
In Somalia, supplies of nutritious food for children under five suffering from moderate malnutrition will run out as soon as July, as the WFP faces an 89 per cent funding gap in the country.
"We are running out of food. The food is not available for distribution, and the ones who will experience the impact of this are going to be very vulnerable children," said Jean-Martin Bauer, the director of WFP's Food Security and Nutrition Analysis Service.
The situation is being worsened by supply chain issues, with fewer ships stopping in Somalia because of disruptions which have affected shipping in the Indian Ocean.
Some WFP stocks have also been held up in Salalah Port in Oman, causing critical delays. Soaring jet fuel prices are also leading to higher operational costs for the United Nations Humanitarian Air Service, the only means to safely access hard-to-reach areas, the WFP said.
In Afghanistan, surging fuel prices have driven up aid transport costs as much as fivefold, and delivery times have shot up from 10 days to as many as 75 days as trucks had to use alternative corridors, the WFP said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit weighed down by rising fuel prices, geopolitical risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar on Friday amid cautious sentiment over lingering geopolitical uncertainty in West Asia and rising fuel prices.
At 6pm, the local currency depreciated to 4.0280/0320 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 4.0095/0140.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the stalemate in the US-Iran negotiations could keep fuel prices elevated and result in higher inflationary pressures.
He said authorities across Asia have also reaffirmed their commitment to stabilising their respective currencies amid heightened market volatility.
He added that the US dollar-Japanese yen pair had surpassed the 160-yen psychological level, prompting the Japanese government to signal its readiness to intervene in the foreign exchange market, while India is mulling scrapping the capital gains tax on bonds to lure foreign investors.
"Hence, the currency market is likely to remain guarded," he told Bernama.
The ringgit also traded lower against a basket of major currencies.
It eased against the British pound to 5.4233/4287 from 5.3872/3932, slid against the euro to 4.6882/6928 from 4.6610/6663, and slipped versus the Japanese yen to 2.5183/5209 from 2.5081/5111 at yesterday's close.
The local currency traded lower against regional peers.
It retreated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1390/1424 from 3.1241/1279, and eased against the Thai baht to 12.3433/3605 from 12.2708/2903.
It also weakened against the Philippine peso to 6.55/6.56 from 6.50/6.52, and dropped against the Indonesian rupiah at 223.3/223.6 versus 222.1/222.4 at Thursday's close.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang Sultan urges on-site gyms for uniformed personnel
Article:
KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang has called on uniformed agencies to incorporate gyms within their facilities to help personnel maintain physical fitness and perform their duties effectively.
Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said personnel from the police, armed forces, navy, air force and Fire and Rescue Department should make full use of such facilities to stay in optimal health.
"Uniformed government agencies, especially frontline services, should provide on-site facilities that enable personnel to improve their well-being and fitness. The aim is to ensure they remain healthy and physically fit.
"This will allow them to incorporate exercise into their daily routines and maintain the fitness needed to carry out their duties whenever required," he said after opening the Mahkota Gym at the Indera Mahkota Fire and Rescue Station here today.
The Ruler said uniformed personnel, regardless of age, should actively use available equipment and participate in sports and recreational activities.
"Many elderly people remain healthy and energetic not because they follow modern fitness trends, but because they have stayed physically active throughout their lives through activities such as gardening, walking and manual work.
"If such an active lifestyle is combined with a more structured and consistent exercise routine, it can further improve fitness, promote better health and help people remain active as they age," he said.
Also present were Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad and Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 5, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today as bargain hunting continued after the recent strong sell-off.
Buying support may continue to support the local market, but investors are likely to stay selective due to geopolitical risks and global economic uncertainty.
The index is expected to trade between 1,690 and 1,710 next week.
Meanwhile, the ringgit fell against the greenback today to 4.0260.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin fell to RM252,289.
Ethereum declined to RM6,791, while Solana was down at RM264.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zeekr partners DC Handal to expand EV charging network in Malaysia
Article:
ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Malaysia has signed a pact with Handal Green Mobility Sdn Bhd (DC Handal) to collaborate on expanding access to high-speed electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure nationwide.
It is aimed at improving charging accessibility for more than 4,000 Zeekr vehicle owners here as the company seeks to strengthen its after-sales ecosystem beyond vehicle sales.
Zeekr said it will leverage DC Handal's existing network of direct current (DC) fast-charging stations located across key transport routes.
The chargers currently offer output of up to 400kW, with future systems potentially supporting up to 640kW as infrastructure capacity increases.
In the initial phase, both parties plan to roll out at least seven charging sites along the North-South Expressway corridor.
Each site will include a minimum of two charging points with at least 120kW capacity.
Further expansion is expected in subsequent phases, although no timeline was disclosed.
The collaboration is part of Zeekr's broader "Zeekr Power" strategy, which focuses on integrating charging infrastructure with vehicle ownership to support longer-distance travel and daily usage.
DC Handal currently operates about 250 charging points across Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah and Sarawak, and has been expanding its presence in highway and urban locations.
DC Handal chief technical officer Esa Fawsal Salleh said the partnership will help improve charging accessibility and support the wider adoption of EVs in Malaysia.
"This collaboration with Zeekr Malaysia allows us to play a central role in supporting Malaysia's sustainable mobility sector by expanding its high-speed EV charging infrastructure and providing our customers with a speedy and seamless charging experience," he added.
Zeekr Malaysia general manager Eddy Lu said the partnership is intended to improve charging convenience for customers as EV adoption grows.
"This collaboration with DC Handal allows us to enhance our Zeekr Power ecosystem by expanding access to high-quality charging infrastructure at key locations," he added.
Zeekr Power is the company's energy and charging arm, which operates ultra-fast charging networks in China and is being positioned for international expansion.
According to the company, its network in China includes hundreds of high-speed charging stations and access to third-party charging infrastructure across major cities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN too divided for expansion, says Puad
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas' proposed Perikatan Nasional Plus (PN+) formula is unrealistic political rhetoric that is unlikely to materialise amid ongoing tensions within the coalition, said Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi.
He described the idea of an expanded coalition as "mere rhetoric", citing existing fractures within Perikatan Nasional (PN), including the emergence of the Internal Political Reform (IPR) movement.
"PN+ is nothing more than unrealistic political rhetoric. Only those who are blind would be misled," he told the New Straits Times.
Puad said internal divisions within PN would make it difficult for the coalition to function effectively, let alone expand into a broader alliance.
He said the proposal comes ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections, where competition over seat allocations is expected to intensify.
"This will trigger disputes over seat distribution, and later extend to negotiations over positions and leadership roles."
Puad added that Umno had already taken a clear stance that it does not trust Pas, particularly in relation to its leadership role within PN.
He said Pas was likely to have its own political agenda in advancing the PN+ concept.
Earlier, it was reported that Pas is exploring the
possibility of reviving the PN+ formula,
an expanded coalition involving existing component parties as well as new political groups aligned with the opposition bloc.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the proposal was among recommendations made during the party's recent Syura Council meeting.
Tuan Ibrahim, who is also PN deputy chairman, said the proposal was among several suggestions aimed at strengthening and improving the coalition.
It is understood that the Syura Council recommended that the "Reset" movement led by former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin be included in PN+.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DayOne closes US$4.5bil Series C as data centre expansion accelerates
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore-based digital infrastructure platform DayOne Data Centers has completed its Series C equity fundraising, securing US$4.5 billion in gross proceeds.
The round was led by existing investors Coatue and Hillhouse, which are now the company's largest shareholders.
New participants include the Indonesia Investment Authority and Achi Capital Partners.
Founded in 2022, DayOne said it has secured more than 1.5 gigawatts (GW) of data centre capacity bookings across Asia Pacific and Europe, positioning it among the fastest-growing global digital infrastructure platforms.
The company said the latest funding will support expansion across key markets including Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Japan, Hong Kong, Finland and Spain.
DayOne added that it may explore additional equity or debt financing in both private and public markets, depending on funding requirements and market conditions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Eita Resources bags RM40.93mil contract for Johor data centre
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Eita Resources Bhd has secured a RM40.93 million contract via its subsidiary Eita Power System Sdn Bhd for the supply and delivery of busduct systems for a data centre project in Johor.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its subsidiary has accepted a letter of award from a third party identified as Company A for the project.
The contract runs from May to September this year.
Eita said the contract is expected to contribute to its earnings for the financial year ending Sept 30, 2026.
It added that the contract will not have any impact on its share capital or shareholding structure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH-BN solo contests in Johor, Negri seen as key test ahead of future polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan's (PH) and Barisan Nasional's (BN) decision to contest separately in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections is expected to test each coalition's grassroots strength and voter support, say political analysts.
They added that the outcome could also signal the future direction of ties between the two partners ahead of other potential state polls, with Sarawak and Melaka widely expected to head to the polls soon.
Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said the prospect of PH and BN contesting separately in future state elections, and even the 16th General Election (GE16), was becoming increasingly realistic.
He said Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun's recent statement that PH intended to contest all 36 seats reflected the coalition's intention to assess its own machinery and support base without relying on BN.
"PH wants to test its own strength, grassroots machinery and voter support without relying on BN," he said.
However, he said that state polls often prioritised local political calculations and organisational survival, while general elections might still incentivise cooperation under the first-past-the-post system.
"If PH and BN contest separately in Malay-majority and mixed constituencies, both risk splitting votes and inadvertently benefiting Perikatan Nasional (PN)," he told the New Straits Times.
Tawfik said the unity government was not on the verge of collapse, despite signs of growing electoral competition between its component coalitions.
"What we are witnessing is the gradual normalisation of competition between coalition partners. PH and BN are governing partners in Putrajaya, but increasingly electoral competitors in several states. While unusual, the arrangement is not necessarily unsustainable," he said.
He added that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's remarks suggesting that discussions remained open indicated that communication between the coalitions was still intact.
"The more likely scenario is a 'cooperate nationally, compete locally' model, where PH and BN work together at federal level while contesting each other in selected state elections," he said.
On the possibility of an early general election, Tawfik said GE16 was unlikely as the federal government still commanded a stable majority and Anwar had little incentive to seek a fresh mandate.
International Islamic University Malaysia political scientist Associate Professor Dr Syaza Shukri said both coalitions appeared keen to test their strength in states where they believed they held an advantage.
"I think they all want to test first and see how it goes by going solo in these first two states. There's confidence in BN in Johor and the same for PH in Negri Sembilan," she said.
However, she added that the dynamics could shift in states where cooperation was more critical.
"In a state like Perak, it is riskier to go solo because it gives PN a big advantage," she said.
She added that the outcome of the state polls could influence strategies heading into GE16.
Syaza also said repeated state elections within a short period could increase public pressure for electoral consolidation.
"There will be pressure to consolidate elections to save costs and respond to public fatigue. Many would prefer a system where elections are held together every four or five years," she said.
Meanwhile, Universiti Utara Malaysia political analyst Prof Dr Mohd Azizuddin Mohd Sani said it was highly likely that coalitions would contest future state elections independently.
However, he warned that three-cornered contests involving PH, BN and PN could again result in hung state assemblies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BYD updates Atto 3 as EV adoption gains traction in Malaysia
Article:
BYD Sime Motors has introduced the updated 2026 BYD Atto 3 at the Sime Motors MotorExpo 2026, as the model enters a refreshed phase in Malaysia's expanding electric vehicle (EV) market.
The launch comes as BYD strengthens its position in the local EV segment, having surpassed 32,000 cumulative vehicle sales across its model range, according to BYD Sime Motors.
BYD's current line-up in Malaysia includes models such as the Dolphin, Atto 3, Seal, M6, Sealion 7, Atto 2 and Seal 6, covering entry-level to larger family-oriented segments.
BYD Malaysia managing director Jacob Ma said the Atto 3 has played a central role in the brand's early growth in the country, describing it as the company's first model introduced locally and one that has since become a top-selling EV SUV.
He said the updated version builds on the global performance of more than 1.1 million units sold and offers improvements in range, usability and driving refinement, supported by continued collaboration with Sime Motors.
BYD Sime Motors managing director Adeline Lew said the Atto 3 helped accelerate awareness of EVs in Malaysia following its introduction in late 2022 and has since become one of the country's best-selling EV models.
Updated variants and specifications
The 2026 BYD Atto 3 is offered in two variants - Ultra and Premium - both equipped with BYD's Blade Battery technology and driver assistance systems.
The Premium variant offers a 74.8 kWh battery with up to 600km NEDC range and accelerates from 0–100 km/h in 5.5 seconds. The Ultra variant features a 60.48 kWh battery with up to 480km range.
Fast-charging capabilities vary between 110kW and 220kW depending on variant.
Prices are set at RM125,800 for the Ultra and RM138,800 for the Premium.
To mark the launch, BYD is offering an introductory package for early buyers, including overtrade incentives and either a wall charger with installation or a six-year service package for selected customers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myra Embun targets suburban demand shift in Putrajaya fringe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Oriental Interest Bhd, through its residential property arm Myra, has launched Myra Embun mixed development in Jenderam, Selangor as demand in the housing market shifts towards more connected suburban locations.
Set on 2.06 hectares of freehold land, the project comprises serviced residences, retail components and a boutique hotel.
It is positioned as one of the earlier integrated developments in the Jenderam area, located about 5km from Putrajaya and linked via the MEX, PLUS and Lingkaran Putrajaya highways.
The launch comes amid a more selective residential market, with National Property Information Centre data showing buyers increasingly prioritising connectivity, functionality and integrated developments, particularly in stratified housing segments.
Myra Embun is located within the Putrajaya-Cyberjaya-Dengkil growth corridor, which continues to see spillover demand from surrounding townships.
The development features two residential towers with 594 serviced apartment units across five layout types ranging from 700 sq ft to 1,490 sq ft. Indicative prices start from about RM250,000 and go up to RM680,000.
The project also includes retail lots, F&B outlets, a supermarket, EV charging facilities and a boutique hotel, alongside shared amenities such as co-working spaces, a multipurpose hall and recreational facilities.
Myra said the development is intended to cater to first-time buyers, upgraders and young families seeking larger unit sizes, amid a broader trend towards smaller new launches in the market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UMS Integration eyes Vietnam precision engineering push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: UMS Integration Ltd has signed a memorandum of understanding with two founding shareholders in Vietnam to explore a proposed restructuring and investment in precision engineering businesses.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the deal involves Viet Nguyen Mechanical Precision Co Ltd, Central Metal Mechanical Co Ltd and Global Metal Plating Co Ltd, which are expected to be consolidated into a newly-formed entity.
Under the deal, UMS Integration plans to invest about US$3.6 million to subscribe for shares in the new company, with an indicative post-deal stake of around 51.6 per cent, subject to due diligence and final valuation.
The targeted businesses are involved in metal processing, plating and precision engineering, primarily serving the semiconductor and related industries in Vietnam.
UMS Integration said the proposed transaction aligns with its strategy to expand its manufacturing footprint in Southeast Asia.
It added that the deal remains subject to definitive agreements and may not materialise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: MyKhas access suspension not arbitrary, rakyat not affected
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The suspension of access to the MyKhas system involving two members of Parliament was not arbitrary, and likely due to specific circumstances, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
The unity government spokesman said the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister's Department had clarified that residents in the affected constituencies could still submit applications as usual, with approvals processed directly by the agency.
"The issue relates to MyKhas allocations. It does not affect the rights, allowances or other benefits provided to elected representatives through Parliament.
"So there must have been factors that led to the restriction. However, the ICU has stated that residents in both constituencies can continue to submit applications, which will be reviewed and approved by the unit," he said at the post-cabinet briefing.
He added that there was no disruption to applications or the disbursement of MyKhas allocations.
MyKhas is an online system managed by ICU to administer parliamentary constituency development funds allocated by the federal government to MPs.
The issue arose after
Subang MP Wong Chen and Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung said their offices had lost access to the portal
.
PKR deputy secretary-general Bryan Ng defended the decision, saying the
suspension was neither an administrative error nor a targeted move, but an institutional consequence linked to the MPs' political positions
.
The
matter stemmed from their attendance at the May 17 launch of Parti Bersama Malaysia
, taken over by
former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad
after leaving the party.
In a separate statement, ICU assured that all allocations meant for the public would continue to be channelled directly, ensuring no disruption to residents' welfare.
It said it had taken note of concerns raised by several MPs over access to allocations under the MyKhas system, adding that all funds for the rakyat would continue to be distributed and managed directly by the unit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nigerian student discharged over cannabis possession charge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Nigerian student was acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court in Petaling Jaya today of a charge of possessing 54.39 grammes of cannabis after the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against him.
Judge Dr Syahliza Warnoh ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case at the close of its case and ordered that the 35-year-old accused be acquitted and discharged without being called to enter his defence.
The accused, who arrived in Malaysia in July 2024 as a student, had been charged with possessing 54.39g of cannabis at about 10.30pm on Nov 29, 2024.
According to the charge, the offence was allegedly committed near pillar XW7 at the basement parking area of the NZX Commercial Centre in Ara Jaya, Jalan PJU 1A/41B, Kelana Jaya, Petaling Jaya.
He was charged under Section 6 of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and punishable under Section 39A(2) of the same Act.
Deputy public prosecutor Ahmad Loqman Hakim Ahmad Khairie appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by lawyers S. Vinesh and T. Daneish.
In delivering her decision, Syahliza held that the prosecution had failed to prove the essential elements of the charge to warrant the accused being called to enter his defence.
The court subsequently ordered that the accused be acquitted and discharged.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Guan Eng: Karpal Singh reclamation project was direct award, not open tender
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Lim Guan Eng claims that the 70-acre Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project was directly awarded and not subjected to an open tender process, escalating a tit-for-tat with Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
The former Penang chief minister said Chow's remarks effectively supported his earlier claim that the reclamation was not included in the Joint Development Agreement (JDA) signed on Feb 21, 2020.
He alleges that the omission indicated the project was not carried out via an open tender or request-for-proposal process by the Penang Development Corporation (PDC).
"The failure to mention the 70-acre sea reclamation in the JDA exposes that it was not carried out through an open tender," he said in a statement.
The Penang government had earlier denied Lim's claim that
the project
was awarded without an open tender.
Lim alleges that the 70-acre reclamation was approved by the state executive council on Aug 25, 2021, 18 months after the JDA was signed, and challenged authorities to show any clause covering the additional acreage.
"If it cannot be shown, then it is clear the reclamation was a direct award on Aug 25, 2021," he claimed.
He also alleged that the project carried significant implications, including land value, environmental impact, and public objections, with the reclaimed land potentially worth RM1.8 billion based on RM600 per sq ft.
Separately, Lim questioned amendments to a RM20 million project management fee involving PLB Engineering and PDC, claiming the state executive council had altered the JDA on Nov 13, 2024, and Apr 9, 2025, allowing deferred payments.
He said it was up to the public to assess whether the revised terms complied with the original tender requirements and JDA.
Lim also called for full transparency from the state government and implementing agencies, stressing that the issue involved major financial, environmental and public interest considerations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two bomb threats in 48 hours: Taylor's campus declared safe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Taylor's University Lakeside Campus was declared safe this morning after a bomb threat prompted an evacuation and a comprehensive security sweep by authorities.
The university said it activated its emergency response procedures immediately upon receiving the threat, with campus access restricted while security teams and police conducted checks across the premises.
"All students, staff and visitors were evacuated while the Subang Jaya district police headquarters carried out a comprehensive inspection.
"Following the assessment, police confirmed that no threat was found and that the campus is safe for normal operations to resume," it said in a statement.
Earlier, social media posts showed students leaving the premises and the presence of a police bomb disposal unit during the operation.
The New Straits Times has reached out to police for confirmation and further details.
View on Threads
The incident came
a day after a bomb threat
at the Sunway campus of Monash University Malaysia prompted an evacuation and security sweep by police.
Police later confirmed that no suspicious objects were found, and investigations into the threat are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EC receives notice on dissolution of Negri Sembilan state assembly
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Election Commission (EC) has received official notification of the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state assembly.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the notice, dated today, triggered the process for a state election, which must be held within 60 days.
"In this regard, a special meeting to discuss the key dates for the state election will be held at a later date," he said in a statement today.
Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced yesterday that the state assembly would be dissolved after obtaining the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
The move followed weeks of political uncertainty after 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen withdrew support for Aminuddin over his handling of the state's royal crisis.
The withdrawal saw Aminuddin lose majority support in the 36-seat state assembly.
Yesterday, Aminuddin also announced that Pakatan Harapan would contest all 36 seats in the coming election.
In the last state election, Pakatan Harapan and Barisan Nasional won a combined 31 of the assembly's 36 seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election polling date to be known next week
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Election Commission (EC) will convene a special meeting next Friday to discuss matters related to the upcoming 16th Johor election.
EC secretary,  Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus, said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun to decide key dates for the polls, such as the nomination and polling dates.
The meeting, he said, would also discuss the electoral roll and other preparations ahead of the polls.
"The EC has officially received notice from the Johor assembly speaker, Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi, on the dissolution of the 15th state assembly on June 2, 2026, as provided under Article 23(2) of Part Two, Johor State Constitution 1895.
Under Article 23 (4) Part Two of the Johor State Constitution 1895, an election must be held within 60 days of the date of the assembly's dissolution," he said in a statement.
Khairul Shahril said a press conference would be called as soon as the meeting concludes.
In a separate statement, Khairul Shahril said applications for postal voting, both in Malaysia and abroad, for the polls are officially open.
He said this involved postal vote applications for all categories, namely Category 1A (Police, armed forces, election workers, EC personnel, and media personnel); Category 1B (Malaysians living abroad); and Category 1C (agencies or organisations).
"All postal vote applications must be done online at https://myspr.spr.gov.my. Those who qualify and wish to become postal voters must register or have a valid EC account before applying.
"The closing date for postal vote applications for all categories will be announced soon. Any inquiries can be directed to 03-88927080 or email upup@spr.gov.my," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold heads for weekly loss on fading Middle East peace hopes
Article:
BENGALURU: Gold edged lower on Friday, and was headed for a weekly loss, as fading hopes of a swift resolution to the Middle East conflict fuelled fears of higher inflation and elevated interest rates.
Spot gold was down 0.2 per cent at US$4,463.73 per ounce at 0849 GMT. The metal has fallen about 1.6 per cent so far this week.
US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.3 per cent to US$4,491.
The Iran-backed Hezbollah militia rejected a new ceasefire in Lebanon and Israel said it would not withdraw troops from the country, undermining US President Donald Trump's efforts to halt fighting there to forge peace with Tehran.
"When the Iran-US negotiations don't seem to be going in the right direction, it tends to drive up oil prices. That triggers inflation fears and increases the likelihood that interest rates will remain relatively high, pressuring gold," said Nitesh Shah, commodity strategist at WisdomTree.
Brent crude oil prices have risen 2.8 per cent so far this week as the key Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut. Bullion has fallen about 16 per cent since the Iran conflict began in late February as oil prices rose, stoking fears of inflation and higher interest rates.
While gold is seen as a hedge against inflation, higher rates tend to weigh on the non-yielding metal.
Markets are currently pricing in a Federal Reserve rate hike before year-end, with a 37 per cent chance of a 25 basis point hike in December, according to CME Group's FedWatch tool.
Investors now await May US nonfarm payrolls data, due later in the day, to gauge the Fed's monetary policy path.
"The main driver of the weaker gold price is lacklustre investment demand – and that is influenced by the US economy still expanding at a solid pace, while inflation is trending higher. The payroll data will be an important input factor for the Fed," said UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo.
Meanwhile, gold demand was subdued in India this week as buyers stayed on the sidelines due to volatile overseas prices, while premiums in China eased.
Spot silver fell 1.7 per cent to US$72.58 per ounce, platinum dropped 1.2 per cent to US$1,877.82, and palladium slid 1.5 per cent to US$1,300.90. They were all headed for a weekly loss.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] BN ready for Negri Sembilan polls, open to unity govt talks
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional says it is prepared to contest the Negri Sembilan election following the dissolution of the state assembly under the Pakatan Harapan-led administration.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the coalition remained open to continuing cooperation within the unity government in the state.
"We are ready to face the election and are open to maintaining the unity government in Negri Sembilan," he said.
"However, it is still too early for us to hold consultations to determine whether the unity government there will be maintained," he told reporters after a walkabout at Hari TVET Negara 2026 at Dataran Putrajaya.
Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the
state assembly would be dissolved
today to pave the way for an election, which must be held within 60 days.
He said the dissolution was carried out after receiving the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, during an audience earlier.
Aminuddin also said seat allocations had been finalised, with Pakatan Harapan set to contest all 36 state seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yen pinned near intervention zone, dollar boosted by Gulf tensions
Article:
LONDON: The Japanese yen tested the 160-per-dollar barrier on Friday, drawing sharp official warnings, as the dollar held firm ahead of key US employment data and Middle East tensions underpinned safe-haven demand.
Peace talks between the US and Iran are at a stalemate, and a reignition of hostilities this week has kept oil above US$90 a barrel, raising risks to global growth.
The yen headed for a fourth straight weekly loss against the dollar, having unwound gains from official buying in late April and early May.
By Friday, it was pressing the 160-per-dollar mark that has triggered intervention in the past, prompting another warning from Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama, who said Japan was ready to respond at any time and reserved the right to take "decisive action" against excessive volatility. The yen was last at 159.93 per dollar.
"Markets are probably a bit reluctant to try to test the BOJ too much" ahead of the US nonfarm payrolls report later Friday, as authorities have shown renewed willingness to intervene, said Khoon Goh, head of Asia research at ANZ.
Despite the risk of intervention, investors have built the largest bearish yen position since July 2024 in recent weeks.
Without a meaningful shift in the outlook for rates and economic growth in Japan, analysts say there is little incentive to unravel those holdings, currently worth nearly US$9 billion, according to LSEG data.
The Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise interest rates this month, as rising energy import costs add to price pressures. Money markets also point to a second hike by year-end.
GULF HOSTILITIES SUPPORT DOLLAR DEMAND
The dollar has been the stand-out in foreign exchange this week, rising about 0.4 per cent against a basket of major currencies and around 1.3 per cent over the past month.
It has been supported by strong US data, expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes and safe-haven demand amid concerns about the impact of higher energy prices on importers such as the euro zone, Japan and China.
Citi's US economic surprise index has hit a three-year high as data on employment, consumer spending and business activity have beaten forecasts, reviving the "American exceptionalism" narrative.
US 10-year Treasury yields have risen 50 basis points since the start of the Iran war, more than those of any other major economy, except Britain where yields are up 66 bps.
"The US is still providing positive economic surprises ... with two-year yields north of 4 per cent, you end up with a scenario where suddenly the conditions for the dollar remain reasonably supportive.
And conversely, from a euro perspective, the perpetuation of elevated energy prices remains a drag on activity there," CIBC Capital Markets head of G10 FX Jeremy Stretch said.
The euro, down 1 per cent over the past month despite expectations of up to three European Central Bank rate hikes this year, was up 0.2 per cent on Friday at US$1.1634. The pound edged up to US$1.345.
Markets now await US nonfarm payrolls later in the day. A Reuters poll forecast an 85,000 rise in jobs in May after a 115,000 increase in April, with the unemployment rate seen steady at 4.3 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Zahid: Umno–Pas talks routine, no involvement of party presidents
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The meeting between Umno and Pas leaders last night did not involve the presidents of either party, says Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the engagement was part of routine political discussions on potential areas of cooperation aimed at preserving political stability.
"The meeting did not involve the presidents of either party. It was merely a discussion on potential areas of collaboration, as we do not want the country's political stability to be undermined.
"We frequently engage in discussions with various parties, particularly our partners in the component parties of Pakatan Harapan.
"In politics, all possibilities remain open because the political landscape is highly dynamic," he told reporters after a walkabout at Hari TVET Negara 2026 at Dataran Putrajaya.
Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional chairman and Deputy Prime Minister, said Umno's doors remained open not only for political dialogue but also for cooperation on national agendas.
"Not only for political discussions, but also for discussions on national agendas that we should jointly translate into action and implement together," he said.
Earlier, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed that leaders from
both parties met
last night.
Pas, Umno leaders held meeting last night, said Hadi
Harakahdaily reported that Hadi, however, said he was not involved, describing it as a routine political meeting, adding: "We meet anyone who wants to meet us."
Asked whether the meeting was aimed at reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN), Hadi said it was merely a proposal that should be viewed in a broader political context.
Meanwhile, Zahid said there had been no discussions on reviving MN 2.0, though he did not rule out future political cooperation.
"There have been no discussions on that. However, all possibilities remain open, whether through new avenues or by fostering a new understanding between parties.
"Nevertheless, it is still too early for us to make any definitive conclusions.
"What is important is that BN and PH, together with our partners from Sarawak and Sabah, have formed the unity government.
"The priority is ensuring stability so the government can serve through the full 15th Parliamentary term," he said.
On June 1, Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man had called for the revival of Muafakat Nasional (MN 2.0), proposing a broader alignment involving Umno, the "Reset" bloc, and other Malay-based parties including Pejuang, Berjasa, Iman and Perkasa.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blockbuster SpaceX IPO set to test high-flying US stocks rally
Article:
NEW YORK: The long-awaited, massive SpaceX initial public offering is expected next week, a major event for the US stock market, with investors wary of possible overexuberance after a stunning rally.
Investors will also assess fresh inflation data and earnings reports from key companies in the technology sector, which has driven the market's recent surge.
The benchmark S&P 500 was on pace for a slim weekly rise, building on nine straight weeks of gains.
Disappointing results late on Wednesday from chip company Broadcom sapped some of the momentum for semiconductor shares, which have boomed on AI optimism.
Indexes remained near record highs, with the S&P 500 up about 11 per cent in 2026, including a nearly 20 per cent rebound since its late-March low for the year.
"Nothing has stuck in terms of pessimism in the last two months," said Mark Hackett, chief market strategist for Nationwide.
"There is just this underpinning of momentum, this insatiable appetite for tech holdings and just the technical buying spree that is really dwarfing almost all other inputs."
Some investors have been bracing for a pause, if not a pullback, after the sharp rally. Risks include the US-Israeli war with Iran and the potential for renewed spikes in energy prices if Middle East tensions flare.
SPACEX IN SPOTLIGHT
Elon Musk's SpaceX is aiming to raise US$75 billion, the most ever for an IPO, in a deal that would value it at US$1.75 trillion. Pricing is expected on June 11, with trading to begin on the Nasdaq the next day.
The company has an unusual and diverse set of businesses, including rockets, satellite communications and AI computing. Adding in the involvement of Musk — Tesla's leader and the world's wealthiest man — and SpaceX's valuation is tricky to pin down, rising to exorbitant levels by some measures.
The company posted a net loss of US$4.94 billion in 2025, even as revenue rose 33 per cent to US$18.67 billion.
The IPO could lure significant attention from retail investors and provide another high-profile way to gain exposure to the AI trade.
"We've got one of the biggest IPOs in history coming ... which I think is the focus of everybody's interest," said Jason Pride, chief of investment strategy and research at Glenmede. "The question mark surrounding it is whether it's an indication of market froth."
SpaceX's debut is expected to be followed by other mega IPOs in the coming months from Anthropic and OpenAI, two of the AI leaders. Anthropic, which makes the Claude chatbot, said this week it has confidentially filed for a US IPO.
The SpaceX IPO is "an important benchmark," said Matt Wittmer, a portfolio manager at Allspring Global Investments, adding that "the company itself will be playing in some of those key areas that people are looking for to find new secular growth opportunities."
CPI DATA DUE, ORACLE, ADOBE TOO
The May Consumer Price Index, due on Wednesday, will show how surging oil and gasoline prices are influencing inflation. One concern is the extent to which higher energy prices might be affecting other CPI components, Pride said, ahead of the Federal Reserve's meeting this month.
"The Federal Reserve is going to be watching this like a hawk," Pride said. "They're going to want to see those pieces continue to remain stable and not increase as a pass-through from the energy and food prices."
In the wake of the spike in energy prices, futures are factoring in a greater chance the Fed raises interest rates this year rather than cuts, after markets had anticipated equity-friendly rate decreases at the start of 2026.
Other economic data next week includes Thursday's report on producer prices. Quarterly reports from tech companies Oracle and Adobe will also be in focus.
Tech has long dominated the US stock market, but the sector's recent outperformance pushed it to more than 39 per cent of the S&P 500's market capitalisation this week, its highest share on record.
The results will test the strength of the tech trade and the rebound in the software industry, which was hit hard to start the year on concerns about AI disruptions. Shares of Oracle are up about 21 per cent this year, while Adobe is down 26 per cent.
"Getting more data points from some of the AI value chain is going to be important," Wittmer said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No discussions on reviving Muafakat Nasional 2.0, says Zahid
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today dismissed suggestions of reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN) 2.0, saying there have been no discussions on the matter between Umno and PAS.
“There have been no discussions on that,” he told reporters after visiting the exhibition site for National Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Day 2026 at Dataran Putrajaya here today.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said Umno and Barisan Nasional’s (BN) priority remains ensuring political stability through the unity government formed with Pakatan Harapan (PH) and parties from Sabah and Sarawak.
“The most important thing is to ensure that the current government remains intact until the end of the 15th Parliament’s term,” he said.
Yesterday, PAS Youth proposed that Umno revive political cooperation through Muafakat Nasional ahead of the upcoming Johor state election.
On a separate matter, Ahmad Zahid said a political meeting involving Umno and PAS leaders held last night did not involve the presidents of either party and was focused on political collaboration and national issues.
He said politics is dynamic and BN remains open to political discussions and deliberations on national matters in the interest of preserving the country’s stability. — Bernama

Title: Pahang Sultan graces launch of gym for firefighters at Indera Mahkota Station
Article:
KUANTAN, June 5 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah today officiated the opening of the Mahkota Gymnasium at the Indera Mahkota Fire and Rescue Station, a reflection of His Royal Highness’ concern for the fitness and well-being of rescue personnel.
The Pahang Ruler arrived at about 3pm and was welcomed by Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department director-general Nor Hisham Mohammad, Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim and Universal Fitness & Leisure Sdn Bhd (UFL) managing director Datuk Radha Krishnan.
The Sultan later received a briefing from Mohd Razam on the development and operation of the gymnasium, which is equipped with exercise equipment donated by His Royal Highness.
Speaking to reporters, Al-Sultan Abdullah said the donation of the fitness equipment was intended to provide firefighters, as part of the nation's front-line workforce, with more comprehensive training facilities and to ensure they remain in peak physical condition while carrying out their duties.
“This facility enables them to train more thoroughly and intensively so that they remain fit and ready to fulfil their responsibilities in assisting those in need of help,” the Sultan said.
Al-Sultan Abdullah also expressed appreciation for the vital role played by firefighters in assisting the public, particularly during emergencies and various incidents.
“The fire and rescue force is undoubtedly a very important front-line service for the state of Pahang. Whenever the people face problems, they are among the agencies that arrive at the scene and provide assistance, whether involving fires, accidents, or even animal disturbances such as snakes.
“My hope is to see members of the security and emergency services remain healthy and physically fit,” His Royal Highness added.
At the same time, the Sultan of Pahang encouraged the public to continue practicing a healthy lifestyle by actively participating in sports and recreational activities to maintain good fitness and health.
During the event, Al-Sultan Abdullah also toured the gymnasium facilities and interacted with the firefighters, affectionately referred to as the brigade’s “muscle men” (abang sado).
The Mahkota Gymnasium, which operates daily from 9am to 10pm, is equipped with 15 pieces of fitness equipment, including treadmills, spinning bikes, leg press machines, a Smith machine, battle ropes, and dumbbells. — Bernama

Title: BN open to continuing unity government with Pakatan in Negeri Sembilan, says Zahid
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is ready for the Negeri Sembilan state election and remains open to continuing its cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH) to retain the unity government in the state, its chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today.
However, Ahmad Zahid said it was still too early to hold formal discussions on any electoral cooperation between the two coalitions.
“We are prepared for the Negeri Sembilan state election and, of course, remain very open to continuing the unity government in the state,” he told reporters after visiting the exhibition site for National Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Day 2026 at Dataran Putrajaya here today.
The deputy prime minister’s remarks came a day after Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution of the 36-seat State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for the 16th state election.
In the 2023 state election, PH and BN formed the Negeri Sembilan government after securing a combined 31 of the 36 seats contested.
PH won 17 seats, followed by BN with 14, while Perikatan Nasional (PN) secured five seats.
Negeri Sembilan became the second state to dissolve its legislative assembly for a fresh election after Johor, which dissolved its State Legislative Assembly on Monday. — Bernama

Title: Nearly 600 smoking notices issued in George Town Heritage Zone since 2022, says Penang rep
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 5 — The Penang State Health Department (JKNPP) has issued 597 notices for smoking within the George Town UNESCO World Heritage Site here, through 114 enforcement operations since 2022.
State Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the world heritage site was gazetted as a smoke-free zone since 2015 and is now subjected to the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852).
“Throughout last year, 106 notices were issued. However, that number has increased this year, with 127 notices issued so far, surpassing the previous year’s total.
“In view of this, we will continue to step up monitoring and enforcement to ensure compliance with the stipulated regulations,” he told reporters after launching the Penang Smoke-Free (PENBAR) promotional materials at the George Town World Heritage Site here today.
Gooi said the increase in notices shows some individuals still fail to comply with the ban despite ongoing educational and advocacy efforts.
He said under Act 852, individuals found smoking in smoke-free zones can face a compound fine of RM250 for each offence.
“We understand that alongside continuous public education, enforcement action must be taken to ensure compliance within the George Town World Heritage Site,” he said.
He added that the state government and JKNPP are intensifying public awareness by installing more promotional materials and warning boards.
Gooi said besides the George Town Heritage Site, several other locations in Penang have also been gazetted as smoke-free zones under Act 852, namely the Jalan Utama Municipal Park, Jalan Air Terjun Botanical Gardens, Air Itam Dam, and Teluk Bahang Dam on the island.
On the mainland, the ban covers the Mengkuang Dam in Berapit and the Ampang Jajar Town Park.
Earlier, Gooi and 70 personnel from the JKNPP Enforcement Division distributed campaign leaflets at premises and fitted banners and special sunshades on trishaws to boost tourist awareness of the smoking ban within the heritage zone. — Bernama

Title: EC to hold special meeting on June 12 to discuss Johor state election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — A special meeting to discuss matters related to the 16th Johor state election will be held on June 12, according to the Election Commission (EC).
EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the commission had received notification from Johor State Assembly Speaker Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi regarding the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 2, as provided under Clause 23(2), Part Two of the Johor Constitution 1895.
“Under Clause 23(4), Part Two of the Johor Constitution 1895, a state election must be held within 60 days from the date of the dissolution of the State Assembly.
“The special meeting, to be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun, will discuss key dates for the 16th Johor state election, including the issuance of the election writ, nomination day, polling day, the electoral roll to be used, and other preparations for the conduct of the election,” he said in a statement today.
He added that a press conference would be held immediately after the special meeting.
The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on Monday after receiving the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail.
In a separate statement, Khairul Shahril said applications for domestic and overseas postal voting for the 16th Johor state election have opened online today via the MySPR portal at
https://myspr.spr.gov.my
The facility is available to all postal voting categories: Category 1A (police officers, military personnel, election workers, EC members and officers, and media personnel), Category 1B (Malaysian citizens residing overseas), and Category 1C (agencies and organisations).
“All postal vote applications must be submitted online. Eligible voters who wish to vote by post must register for, or already have, a MySPR account before applying,” he said.
He added that the closing date for postal vote applications for all categories would be announced later.
For enquiries or further information, the public may contact the EC at 03-8892 7080 or email upup@spr.gov.my — Bernama

Title: Four KL cops plead not guilty to extorting salesman of RM10,000
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Four police corporals pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of extorting RM10,000 from a salesman three years ago.
Mohd Al Hafis Sairudin, 43, Mohd Azmi Mohd Ghozali, 40, Muhamad Azlan Zakaria, 39, and Muhammad Hanif Abdul Talib, 30, were jointly charged with extorting Danny Goh Ngee Guan, 35, by allegedly causing him to fear that he would be fined at least RM50,000 by the court.
The alleged act prompted the salesman to hand over RM10,000 at an apartment in Jalan Kepong, Sentul at 12pm on December 23, 2023.
The charge, under Section 384 of the Penal Code read together with Section 34 of the same Act, carries a maximum jail term of 10 years, or a fine, or whipping, or any two of these punishments upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail as the offence is non-bailable, while lawyer Ahmad Ikram Ab Halim, representing all the accused, applied for minimum bail on the grounds that his clients were the sole breadwinners of their families and had fully cooperated during investigations.
Magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan allowed bail of RM8,000 for each accused with two sureties, with additional conditions requiring them to report monthly at the nearest police station and not to disturb witnesses or the victim until the case is concluded.
The court fixed July 8 for mention. — Bernama

Title: Monash, Taylor’s say campuses back to ‘normal’ after bomb scares, confirm cops gave safety clearance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Both Taylor’s University and Monash University Malaysia said this afternoon that their campuses have resumed normal operations, after police confirmed that it was safe to do so.
Earlier today, Taylor’s University campus in Subang Jaya was reportedly evacuated.
In a brief statement posted on its Facebook page around 3pm, Taylor’s University explained it had “activated its emergency response procedures following a reported bomb threat”.
“The campus was immediately evacuated, and access was restricted while authorities conducted the necessary security assessments.
“Following a thorough security sweep, the Subang Jaya District Police have confirmed that the situation is all clear. Taylor’s Lakeside Campus is safe, and normal operations may resume,” it said in the statement.
Taylor’s University thanked its students, staff, visitors, emergency responders and the authorities for their cooperation, swift response and professionalism.
In its latest update this afternoon, Monash University Malaysia said it had “resumed normal campus operations” today, following a thorough security assessment by local authorities in response to yesterday’s reported bomb threat.
The university thanked both the Subang Jaya and Larkin district police and the university’s own security team for their swift response.
“We are continuing to work closely with local authorities to investigate this matter and have temporarily increased security at our campus to ensure the health and safety of our community,” it said, also thanking its community for their patience while work was done to facilitate a safe return to normal campus operations.
Monash University Malaysia this afternoon also posted a link to its website, where it gave a timeline of key developments in response to the reported security incident.
Previously, the university said it had activated emergency response procedures last night following a reported bomb threat, including evacuation of campus and restricted access as precautionary measures while authorities conducted a thorough assessment.
Based on the timeline provided on its website, Monash University Malaysia in an update dated yesterday at 11.53pm said the Subang Jaya police had gave the clearance for its Sunway City campus to resume normal operations.
In the same timeline, the university in an update dated today at 5.51am said police had also given clearance for its CSJB campus in Johor to resume normal operations.

Title: EC receives notice of Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution, says special meeting to set poll dates
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Election Commission (EC) has received official notification of the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly today.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission received a letter from Negeri Sembilan State Assembly Speaker Datuk M K Ibrahim Abd Rahman notifying it of the dissolution of the 15th State Legislative Assembly under Article 56(2) of the state constitution.
He said Article 56(4) of the state constitution requires a state election to be held within 60 days of the dissolution of the assembly.
“Accordingly, a special meeting to discuss the key dates for the Negeri Sembilan state election will be held on a date to be announced later,” he said in a statement today.
Last night, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced at a special press conference at his official residence that the state assembly had been dissolved effective today to pave the way for the 16th state election.
Aminuddin said the dissolution followed the consent of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, during an audience at Istana Besar Seri Menanti. — Bernama

Title: MyKhas suspension does not affect MPs’ rights, says Fahmi
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Government spokesperson Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has stressed that the suspension of access to the MyKhas portal for the Members of Parliament (MPs) for Petaling Jaya and Subang does not affect their rights or functions as elected representatives.
In fact, he said allocations channelled through the MyKhas platform are special funds under the prime minister’s purview and are separate from the facilities and entitlements provided to MPs through Parliament.
The communications minister also said residents in both constituencies would continue to have access to assistance despite the restrictions.
“The Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister’s Department (ICU JPM) has clarified that residents in both parliamentary constituencies may still submit applications, which will be assessed and approved directly by the ICU.
“As such, there is no issue regarding applications or the allocation of funds,” he told a press conference after the Cabinet meeting today.
Fahmi was responding to claims by Subang MP Wong Chen and Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung that their offices had been denied access to the MyKhas system, affecting applications for People-Friendly Projects (PMR) allocations in their constituencies.
Yesterday, the ICU JPM assured the public that all allocations intended for the people would continue to be disbursed and managed directly to ensure that public welfare remains unaffected.
The agency also said it would continue carrying out its coordinating role in an orderly manner to ensure the smooth and effective implementation of government programmes and initiatives for the benefit of the public. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar directs all government digital services to be developed in-house, says Fahmi
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed that all aspects of government digital service development be carried out internally to safeguard data security and sovereignty.
Madani Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, who is also communications minister, said the prime minister conveyed the directive during the Cabinet meeting today.
Fahmi said the internal development of digital services would be coordinated by the Ministry of Digital through the National Digital Department.
“This takes into account data security and sovereignty aspects, direct access to source code, as well as talent development, particularly among civil servants,” he told a post-Cabinet meeting press conference here today.
In another development, Fahmi said the prime minister had instructed that firm action be taken against any party found abusing licences, visas or business facilities in the country.
“We leave it to the relevant authorities to take action in accordance with the law, and we remind all business operators to always comply with regulations and laws, especially those holding licences to conduct business or undertake commercial activities,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Tuanku Muhriz performs Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir in Seri Menanti
Article:
KUALA PILAH, June 5 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today performed Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir in Seri Menanti.
His Royal Highness was accompanied by his sons, Tunku Besar of Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin and Tunku Zain Al-’Abidin.
The prayers were led by Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir's Imam 1, Wan Muhammad Saifuddin Wan Salim.
After the prayers, Tuanku Muhriz attended a luncheon with more than 500 members of the public at Luak Tanah Mengandung. — Bernama

Title: Police arrest 18 men after two Kampung Air fights, one stabbed with stingray spine stable
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 5 — Police have arrested 18 men to assist in investigations into two fights that occurred in Kampung Air early Thursday morning.
In the first incident, at about 4am, a local man in his 20s sustained injuries to his right calf after being stabbed with a sharp object believed to be a stingray spine while at a food outlet in the area.
The victim was then rushed to the Queen Elizabeth II Hospital (HQE II) for treatment and is reported to be in stable condition.
Acting Kota Kinabalu district police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Abdul Rahman said initial investigations found that the incident stemmed from a disagreement between several individuals before escalating into a fight.
He said the victim, who attempted to break up the altercation, was believed to have been attacked by several suspects, resulting in his injuries.
“About an hour later, another fight was reported at the same location, which is believed to be linked to the earlier incident.
“In the second incident, a man sustained facial injuries after being assaulted by several individuals,” he said in a statement.
Syed Lot said police subsequently arrested 18 men believed to have been involved in the incidents.
Police also seized several items believed to be connected to the case, he said, adding that initial urine tests conducted on all suspects returned negative results for drug abuse.
Syed Lot said the case is being investigated under Section 148 of the Penal Code for rioting using weapons and Section 324 of the Penal Code for intentionally causing injury using a weapon or dangerous instrument.
Syed Lot urged members of the public who witnessed the incidents or have relevant information to come forward and assist police by contacting the investigating officer, Insp Movendran A/L Apoorvasamy, at 018-574 6310.
Syed Lot also reminded the public not to speculate or spread unverified information that could disrupt investigations and affect public order.
“The police view seriously any acts of violence and fighting that could threaten public security and order and will take strict action against any individual who violates the law,” he said. — The Borneo Post

Title: Kuching man jailed one month, fined RM1,500 for stealing Wagyu beef and salmon trout at supermarket
Article:
KUCHING, June 5 — A 28-year-old man who stole two Australian Wagyu beef cuts and two salmon trout from a supermarket here was sentenced to one month’s imprisonment and fined RM1,500, in default one month’s jail.
Mohammad Hanim Suita pleaded guilty before Magistrate Ling Hui Chuan to a charge under Section 380 of the Penal Code, which provides for a jail term of up to 10 years and a fine upon conviction.
The offence was committed at a supermarket in a shopping centre along Jalan Canna here at about 12.30am on June 1.
Based on the facts of the case, a complainant at the shopping centre noticed a suspicious-looking man, later identified as the accused, leaving the premises.
The complainant, together with a security guard, subsequently apprehended the man on suspicion of stealing items from the supermarket.
Upon inspection, they found several food items believed to have been stolen in the basket of the man’s motorcycle. The accused was then handed over to the police for further action.
In mitigation, the accused pleaded for a fine-only sentence, saying he had stolen the food items to be resold to get medication for his sexually transmitted infection (STI).
However, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Afiq Safly Nor Kazly urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence commensurate with the offence.
The accused was unrepresented. — The Borneo Post
